cataloging_subject.label: Hymns to the Virgin and Christ, the Parliament of Devils, and Other Religious Poems (Furnivall twenty-six-item selection)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The exact edition-defined Furnivall selection encoded by ANT9911.0001.001: the 1895 reprint title leaf and printed pp. 1–128, comprising three Virgin hymns, eight Christ-centered items, and fifteen other religious items. It is not one medieval work, the whole of Lambeth MS 853, every text Furnivall mentions, or the whole 136-page print carrier; printed pp. 129–136 and all other front/back matter are excluded.

catalog_summary.title: Hymns to the Virgin and Christ, the Parliament of Devils, and Other Religious Poems (Furnivall twenty-six-item selection)
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous medieval authors; Richard Caistre attributed only for one prayer
catalog_summary.editor: Frederick James Furnivall, editor and selector; Early English Text Society, publisher/series body
catalog_summary.date: Medieval and heterogeneous; edited selection first published 1867

titles.value: Hymns to the Virgin & Christ, The Parliament of Devils, and Other Religious Poems

assertion.notes: Exact encoded OCR heading; retained as a variant, not preferred over the printed/cataloged title.

notes: Preferred part title is The Belief, established by printed p. 101 and the Lambeth catalog.

agents.name: Anonymous medieval authors
notes: Selection-level collective only; it does not imply one author or erase the scoped Caistre attribution.

agents.name: Early English Text Society
notes: Publisher and series body; the work-metadata responsibility vocabulary has no publisher role, so this role remains explicit here and in the catalog summary rather than being coerced into authorship.

scope.description: The count-bearing medieval items other than the scoped Caistre attribution
Selection and arrangement of the twenty-six-item edition-defined unit

notes: Wright supplied nineteenth-century transcripts for Furnivall; he is not assigned medieval authorship.

date_statements.display: Medieval; heterogeneous; individual composition dates unresolved

assertion.notes: Witness dates are lineage-scoped and are not normalized as selection-level composition dates.
Part-scoped date based on the edition heading and historical subject; it does not date the selection as a whole.

places.label: England
notes: Broad cultural origin only; not asserted as the composition place of every item.
The Lambeth manuscript dialect region is not generalized into a composition place for all works.

language_statements.label: Latin

scope.description: Eight-line close, five-line preface, refrains, rubrics, and other short Latin matter

notes: Modern editorial English belongs to part:editorial-apparatus and is not exposed as a work-level discovery facet.

form_statements.description: Twenty-five verse works and one prose work.
The one count-bearing prose work; XML L/LG layout does not make it verse.

genre_statements.label: Carol
notes: Part-scoped repertory descriptor; not a textual-form value.

summaries.value: Furnivall’s bounded edition-defined selection brings together twenty-six anonymous or attributed medieval religious items: three hymns to the Virgin, eight Christ-centered items, and fifteen further devotional, moral, eschatological, lament, and historical pieces. Twenty-five are verse and one is prose; Kepe Wel Cristes Comaundement appears in two parallel witness versions but counts once as one intellectual-work family.

Encoded boundary: 1895 reprint title leaf and printed pp. 1–128. Excluded: all other front matter and printed pp. 129–136 (Halle extract, glossary, and index). Modern headings, ARGUMENT, associated prose, and NOTE1 material inside the selected pages are modeled as zero-count editorial apparatus.

Count-once intellectual extent is 3,360 verse lines in 392 groups. Full two-stream serialization/representation is 3,464 verse lines in 405 groups; the additional 104 lines and 13 groups are the second complete Kepe Wel version stream and are retained in null-count analytical children, not re-added to the abstract-work total.

extent.description: Thirty-three content-part records comprise 26 count-bearing intellectual items, three zero-count structural groups, one zero-count editorial-apparatus frame, two null-count analytical Kepe Wel version streams, and one null-count analytical Latin preface. The count-once aggregate is 3,360 lines/392 groups; the full two-stream representation is 3,464 lines/405 groups and appears only here and in supporting evidence.

content_parts.label: Furnivall Editorial Apparatus within the Encoded Selection
content_parts.summary: Modern editorial apparatus encoded inside the selected title-leaf and pp. 1–128 boundary; it is visible as a zero-count prose frame, not a medieval work.

locators.value: encoded title leaf and apparatus within XML nodes 3–19

notes: Zero-count modern editorial frame: encoded title leaf, Furnivall headings, ARGUMENT, associated prose, and NOTE1 apparatus inside the selected boundary. Header and omitted front/back stubs are excluded.

evidence.citation: Pinned General Corpus source XML for ant9911
evidence.summary: The 204,083-byte pinned XML establishes the encoded selection, title leaf, content sequence, serialized L/LG features, editorial apparatus, and U-M header statements.

evidence.citation: Structural and count-boundary audit of the pinned ant9911 XML
evidence.locator: 20 DIV1; 15 DIV2; 33 HEAD; 3,499 L; 407 LG; target title leaf and nodes 3–19
evidence.summary: Direct structural inspection distinguishes 26 count-bearing works from structural, analytical, and editorial records; excludes prose layout, note quotation, trailer, OCR split, and Latin preface from verse totals; and yields 3,464/405 representation versus 3,360/392 count-once extent.

evidence.citation: Furnivall 1895 reprint and 1867 first edition evidence in the paired lineage manifest
evidence.summary: The linked edition evidence supports the selection boundary, title/readings, responsibilities, print locators, publication history, source statements, and Wright transcript intermediaries.

evidence.citation: DIMEV stable v1 primary and 2.0-alpha.1 comparator evidence in the paired lineage manifest
evidence.summary: The linked scholarly evidence supports 25 exact verse records, their witness/source loci, and the part-scoped Bible and carol descriptors without changing work identity or count-once extent.

evidence.citation: Current Lambeth MS 853 catalog evidence in the paired lineage manifest
evidence.summary: The linked catalog evidence supports Lambeth loci, the preferred title The Belief, and the unresolved MS 583/853 distinction.

evidence.citation: Catalog synthesis and direct visual inspection in the paired lineage manifest
evidence.summary: The linked synthesis records the exact edition/witness boundary, preferred-title correction, count hierarchy, cache audit, and modeled uncertainties.

notes: Modern editorial English is confined to part:editorial-apparatus and is not exposed as a selection-level language facet.
The DIMEV v2-alpha descriptor carol is modeled as a part-scoped repertory genre for P31 and P32, never as a prose/verse/mixed form.
Witness dates remain in lineage; no witness date is normalized as the composition date of this heterogeneous edited selection.
